task: Create a new auto-loaded module modules/joi_orchestrator_status.py that registers a Flask blueprint (or attaches a route to the existing app) and exposes GET /orchestrator/status; the route must be read-only and safe for auto-loading by performing a lazy import of modules.joi_orchestrator inside the request handler within a try/except ImportError (no top-level imports or any initialization/re-initialization calls), then use getattr to read boolean properties like is_connected and is_authorized with default False and return a JSON response (e.g., {"status":"ok","connected":..., "authorized":...} or {"status":"unavailable","connected":false,"authorized":false} on ImportError), log errors but do not change any orchestrator state, and include a minimal unit test that requests the endpoint, asserts a 200 response and that invoking the endpoint does not modify the orchestrator module or its state.
